pet.name: Bam Bam
pet.special_needs: Needs secure supervised enrichment, feeding consistency, hydration monitoring, and close observation of stress, appetite, and handling tolerance.
pet.characteristics: Raccoons in captive/companion settings require secure, enriched environments with climbing structures, foraging opportunities, and puzzle feeders to support natural behaviors. Feeding should follow a consistent schedule with varied whole foods; hydration monitoring is essential as they are prone to dehydration. Handling tolerance varies widely by individual and early socialization; stress indicators include excessive vocalization, aggression, or appetite loss and warrant immediate observation. They are intelligent, dexterous, and highly motivated by food, making them escape risks without proper containment. Environmental enrichment, regular veterinary oversight, and close behavioral monitoring are critical for welfare.
pet.species_profile_summary: Raccoons typically live 12-16 years in captive settings and require intensive enrichment, consistent feeding, stress monitoring, and secure housing to thrive as companion animals.

age_context.summary: Raccoons typically live 12-16 years in captive settings and require intensive enrichment, consistent feeding, stress monitoring, and secure housing to thrive as companion animals.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Pets on kidney-support diets often need strict consistency around meals and hydration. Wet-food feeding schedules, water refreshes, and appetite monitoring are common routine tasks that owners can track day by day.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning feeding and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide consistent meal at same time daily; monitor water intake and appetite.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Raccoons require strict feeding consistency and hydration monitoring to prevent dehydration and support overall health.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening feeding and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide consistent meal at same time daily; refresh water; observe appetite and intake.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Second daily feeding maintains consistency and allows tracking of hydration and appetite patterns across the day.

raw_recommendations.name: Behavioral and health observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Record appetite, water intake, energy level, bathroom habits, vocalization, and handling tolerance.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Short, specific observation tasks create a record of stress indicators and health changes for early detection of issues.

raw_recommendations.name: Enrichment and foraging activity
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide puzzle feeders, climbing structures, or foraging opportunities in secure supervised space.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Raccoons are intelligent and dexterous; enrichment supports natural behaviors and mental stimulation without requiring large time blocks.

raw_recommendations.name: Secure containment and escape-risk check
raw_recommendations.notes: Inspect enclosure for gaps, weak points, or damage; ensure all latches and locks are secure.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Raccoons are highly motivated escape risks; daily containment checks prevent injury and loss.

raw_recommendations.name: Stress and handling tolerance assessment
raw_recommendations.notes: Note any excessive vocalization, aggression, appetite loss, or changes in handling tolerance.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Handling tolerance varies widely; early detection of stress indicators guides safe interaction and veterinary follow-up.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly veterinary check-in readiness
raw_recommendations.notes: Compile observation notes; flag any appetite refusal, sudden decline, or behavioral changes for veterinary contact.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent documentation supports early veterinary intervention if multiple meals are refused or health declines.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly enrichment and environment refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Rotate toys, puzzle feeders, and climbing structures; deep clean enclosure; assess environmental security.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Intelligent raccoons benefit from environmental variety; monthly refresh maintains engagement and prevents boredom-related stress.
